extraction.one_line_summary: ASGREEN Kasper bridged solo to EVENPOEL with 1.7km remaining after teammate LAMPART's earlier attack forced selection, won two-up sprint for stage and leader's jersey for EVENPOEL.
tactical_patterns: Deceuninck-Quick Step used LAMPART's attack to force selection before EVENPOEL countered, creating bridge opportunity for ASGREEN 1.7km out.
Trek-Segafredo's PEDERSEN stayed in 7-minute break despite being GC threat at 16:29, indicating teams underestimated time gap threat.
NIBALI attacking for KOM points on final climb despite GC group imploding suggests Bahrain-Merida prioritized jersey over stage control.
ACKERMANN dropped on penultimate climb shows Bora-Hansgrohe lacked climbing domestiques to position their sprinter on rolling finishes.

rider_signals.EVENPOEL Remco: positive: Counter-attacked after LAMPART's move with under 3km to go, gained 13 seconds on GC despite losing sprint to teammate
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: Dropped with 20km remaining and lost overnight lead, catastrophic collapse for expected bunch sprint favorite
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: Bridged solo 1.7km gap to catch EVENPOEL after earlier team moves softened group, exceptional tactical awareness
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: neutral: Took KOM points from 7-minute break but got caught, Trek missed opportunity to defend break with GC threat
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: Dropped on penultimate climb before final circuit, exposed climbing deficit on moderately rolling stages
